pattern: Treating shared emails as edges turns account merging into finding connected components, after which each component's emails are gathered, sorted, and prefixed with the owner's name.
hints: Two accounts belong together whenever they share at least one email, so think of emails as nodes that link the accounts containing them.
Union-find or a graph traversal groups all transitively connected emails into a single component.
Map every email to a representative via union-find, bucket emails by their root, then for each bucket output the name followed by the emails in sorted order.

edge_inputs.description: two Johns merge via a shared email, others stay apart
same name but no shared emails stays separate
chain merge across three accounts via overlapping emails
two unrelated people same email count
duplicate emails within one account dedupe correctly

stress.description: random accounts drawn from a shared email pool to force overlaps and components
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 60))
    pool = ['e{}@m.com'.format(i) for i in range(20)]
    names = ['Al', 'Bo', 'Cy', 'Di']
    accounts = []
    for _ in range(n):
        cnt = rng.randrange(1, 4)
        emails = rng.sample(pool, cnt)
        accounts.append([rng.choice(names)] + emails)
    return [accounts]
